title: podman pull sagemathinc/cocalc results in an infinite extraction of files
body:
How to reproduce (I already downloaded the layers so ignore the "already exists"):

havana ~ > podman pull sagemathinc/cocalc
Trying to pull docker.io/sagemathinc/cocalc...Getting image source signatures
Copying blob b8af9ea7344d skipped: already exists
Copying blob 0330ca45a200 skipped: already exists
Copying blob 471db38bcfbf skipped: already exists
Copying blob 0b4aba487617 skipped: already exists

Copying blob 2190488b7f54 skipped: already exists
Copying blob dce0b5b6ee7f done
Copying config dbd2e2ecff done
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures

Now at this point watch ~/.local/share/containers/storage/vfs/dir growing bigger and bigger. I let it run for hours and stopped around 300GB.

havana ~ > podman --version
podman version 1.3.1

This is tested on openSUSE Tumbleweed as of version 20190604.

comments.body:
Most likely issue here is you are using vfs rather then fuse-overlayfs.

yum install fuse-overlayfs
rm -rf ~/.local/share/container ~/.config/containers

And try 
podman info

Does it show overlay driver?  If yes, try your experiment again.

```
havana ~ > podman info
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.8.2
  Conmon:
    package: podman-1.3.1-1.2.x86_64
    path: /usr/lib/podman/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 0.2.0, commit: '
  Distribution:
    distribution: '"opensuse-tumbleweed"'
    version: "20190604"
  MemFree: 8500428800
  MemTotal: 16734777344
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0~rc8-1.2.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc8
      spec: 1.0.1-dev
  SwapFree: 15871246336
  SwapTotal: 16726884352
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 8
  hostname: havana
  kernel: 5.1.5-1-default
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  uptime: 26h 36m 25.93s (Approximately 1.08 days)
registries:
  blocked: null
  insecure: null
  search:
  - docker.io
store:
  ConfigFile: /suse/idoenmez/.config/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 0
  GraphDriverName: vfs
  GraphOptions: null
  GraphRoot: /suse/idoenmez/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus: {}
  ImageStore:
    number: 0
  RunRoot: /run/user/16908
  VolumePath: /suse/idoenmez/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
```

fuse-overlayfs doesn't seem to be available in openSUSE. But, even with vfs this can't be the expected behaviour.

Depending on how big the originals are, it's possible that this is intended
behavior - it's potentially not infinite extraction, just one copy of each
layer for each running container with the image (plus one for the image
itself). VFS does not support storage deduplication, so any running
containers will cause a full copy of the extracted contents of the image.

That image has a lot of layers, by the looks of it, so 300gb seems at least
plausible, but I'd need to check it's contents to be certain

@saschagrunert @vrothberg PTAL
Any idea why fuse-overlayfs is not built for OpenSUSE?  @ismail One thing I would recommend trying if you want to use VFS is to setup xfs reflink on the homedir.

If this is not easy for you to do after the fact, you could try to allocate a ton of space and mount it as an XFS Driver on your homedir.  Here is what I have experimented with.

```
 cat ~/xfs.sh
truncate -s 10g xfs.img
mkfs.xfs -m reflink=1 xfs.img
mount xfs.img /home/dwalsh/.local/share/containers
```

> @saschagrunert @vrothberg PTAL
> Any idea why fuse-overlayfs is not built for OpenSUSE? @ismail One thing I would recommend trying if you want to use VFS is to setup xfs reflink on the homedir.

I submitted fuse-overlay to openSUSE Tumbleweed, so this bug can be closed as invalid I guess :) Thanks a lot for your help.
So this seems solved to me. I will update the openSUSE podman package to depend on fuse-overlayfs.

Make it a recommends not a requires.  Since podman does not need it to run as root.

Thanks @saschagrunert and @ismail
